varieties.name: 🧬 HYBRID = First-generation cross
varieties.origin: Domesticated from sea beet (Beta vulgaris subsp. maritima) in Mediterranean region. Cultivated for roots since 16th-17th century.

varieties.name: Detroit Dark Red
varieties.traits: Globe-shaped, 5-8cm diameter, smooth dark red skin, uniform roots
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep blood red, fine-grained, ringless, sweet and tender. Low geosmin content (less earthy flavor)
varieties.availability: Diggers Club, Eden Seeds, The Seed Collection, Australian Seed, most major seed suppliers Australia-wide

varieties.name: Chioggia (Candy Stripe)
varieties.origin: Chioggia, Italy, cultivated since 1840s or earlier
varieties.traits: Flattened globe shape, 5-7cm diameter, light red exterior
varieties.flesh_fruit: Distinctive alternating red and white concentric rings (bull's-eye pattern). Sweet, mild, less earthy than red varieties
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection, Diggers Club, Eden Seeds, widely available in Australia

varieties.name: Golden Detroit (Detroit Golden)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Golden yellow-orange, sweet, milder and less earthy than red varieties. Does not bleed or stain
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection, Australian Seed, Succeed Heirlooms, Diggers Club

varieties.name: Burpee's Golden
varieties.origin: USA, introduced by Burpee Seed Company 1940s (marketed 1970)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep golden yellow when cooked, tender, mild, sweet. Does not bleed
varieties.availability: Australian Seed (Burpee Golden), Tassie Seeds, Diggers Club

varieties.name: Cylindra (Formanova, Butter Slicer)
varieties.traits: Long cylindrical shape, 15-20cm long, 4-5cm diameter, dark red skin, smooth
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep crimson, fine-grained, sweet, tender. Uniform slices when cut

varieties.name: Early Wonder (Tall Top)
varieties.origin: USA, introduced 1911 by S.D. Woodruff & Sons
varieties.traits: Semi-globe to flattened globe, 6-8cm diameter, dark red skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep red with light red zoning, sweet, tender
varieties.availability: Eden Seeds, The Lost Seed, Very Edible Gardens (Australian suppliers)

varieties.name: Crosby Egyptian (Early Crosby Egyptian)
varieties.traits: Flattened globe shape, 6-8cm diameter, dark purplish-red skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep red with light red zoning (white zoning in warm weather), sweet

varieties.name: Bull's Blood
varieties.traits: Globe-shaped roots, deep purple skin, compact 30-40cm growth
varieties.availability: Eden Seeds, Very Edible Gardens, The Lost Seed

varieties.name: Ruby Queen
varieties.origin: USA, introduced 1950s by Northrup King. All-America Selections Winner 1957
varieties.traits: Perfect globe shape, 5-7cm diameter, smooth medium-red skin, uniform
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep red, ringless, exceptionally sweet, buttery smooth texture
varieties.availability: International heirloom suppliers (some ship to Australia)

varieties.name: Lutz Green Leaf (Winter Keeper)
varieties.traits: Large semi-tapered roots can reach 15cm+ diameter, glossy green leaves with red stems
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep purple-red, sweet, remains tender even when very large
varieties.yield_notes: Very high - can produce massive roots

varieties.name: Albina Vereduna (Snow White)
varieties.traits: Round to globe, 10-15cm diameter, pure white skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pure white, exceptionally sweet (sweeter than red), mild, delicate
varieties.availability: European seed companies (check Australian import availability)

varieties.name: Melbourne Early Boltardy (Boltardy)
varieties.origin: United Kingdom, adapted for Australian conditions
varieties.traits: Globe-shaped, 6-8cm diameter, deep purple skin, smooth
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep purple, ringless, sweet, smooth texture, delicate taste

varieties.name: Ooty 1
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University (TNAU), India. Bred for Nilgiris hill conditions
varieties.availability: TNAU seed centers, Kerala Agricultural University, local agricultural offices

varieties.name: Detroit Dark Red
varieties.traits: Globe-shaped, 5-8cm diameter, smooth dark red skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep blood red with light red zoning, sweet

varieties.name: Crimson Globe
varieties.traits: Round to flattened globe, medium-sized roots
varieties.flesh_fruit: Medium red exterior, crimson red interior without zonations

varieties.name: Red Ball
varieties.traits: Red ball-shaped, smaller in size than Detroit Dark Red

varieties.name: Early Wonder
varieties.traits: Flat globular shape, dark red skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Dark red with light red zoning
varieties.yield_notes: First beet off the mark - very early

varieties.name: Tropical Beetroot Hybrids
varieties.traits: Bred for heat tolerance and monsoon resistance

varieties.name: All varieties are non-GMO. Heirloom varieties marked with
